observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management, through all concerned offices, ensure the fair presentation of expenses in its financial statements by:

requiring all offices to use appropriate expense accounts in their budget proposals, ensuring that budget submissions align with government accounting and budgeting standards and accurately reflect the nature and purpose of the expenses, thereby preventing misclassification at the budgeting stage;
strengthening the review process of budget proposals by the MBO through careful evaluation of the accounts used in the proposals, ensuring they correspond to the correct expense classifications before finalizing the budget; and
ensuring that the MAO records actual transactions under the proper expense accounts, accurately reflecting their true nature and purpose.

We recommended that Management, through the MAO, ensure the fair presentation of liabilities in the financial statements by:

preparing the necessary adjusting entry to reclassify the amount reimbursed by PhilHealth, in accordance with the Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units; and
ensuring that all transactions are accurately reviewed and classified moving forward, with particular attention to amounts payable to PhilHealth to prevent misclassification.

We recommended that the Management, through the MAO, reflect the proper valuation of its PPEs by providing the proper depreciation of assets and:

prepare the necessary journal entries to adjust the total accumulated depreciation of the accounts noted as of December 31, 2024, to correct the identified deficiencies;
revise the lapsing schedule of the identified accounts to align with the remaining useful life of the main structure; and
conduct a comprehensive review of the lapsing schedules of the Municipality's other PPE components to identify and correct any similar inconsistencies in depreciation.

We recommended that the Management, through the MAO, reflect the proper classification of its asset accounts by:

preparing the necessary journal entries to reclassify the identified assets into their recommended classifications; and
conducting a comprehensive review of the schedules of the Municipality's other PPE components to identify and correct any similar inconsistencies in asset classification.

We recommended that the Management undertake the following actions to facilitate the fair presentation of accounts presented in the financial statements by:

preparing the necessary journal entries to recognize the identified balances of undistributed or undisposed donations as inventories; and
requiring all concerned Municipal offices receiving donations in kind to henceforth maintain, in sufficient detail, a summary/list of donations received and a report and complete documentation of their eventual disposition, to be submitted to the MAO, who shall make the proper accounting thereof in accordance with Paragraphs 96 and 97 of the IPSAS No. 23 and Section 63 of Presidential Decree No. 1445.

We recommended that the Management enforce the implementation of EO No. 4, Series of 2024, to address the persistent deficiencies noted over the cash accountabilities and to reinforce internal controls over the financial and cash management of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Treasurer to strictly observe the daily deposit of collections with the authorized depository bank, in accordance with Section 2 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and Section 88 (A), Book II of the LTOM 2nd Edition to prevent the accumulation of significant balances of undeposited cash collections and strengthen safeguards over government funds.

We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Treasurer to strictly adhere to the rules on the proper granting and liquidation of cash advances in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of P.D. 1445 to prevent the accumulation of unliquidated cash advances.

We also recommended that the Management, through the Municipal Accountant, closely monitor the recording of cash advances granted and their liquidation, and issue demand letters to officials and employees with overdue unliquidated accounts to enforce timely liquidation.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to maintain the prescribed cashbooks for cash advances and to conduct regular reconciliations with accounting records at the end of every month or as needed, in order to establish specific balances of his accountabilities and to promptly make the necessary adjustments of discrepancies noted, and ensure the reliability and accuracy of financial reports, in accordance with the provisions of the LTOM.

We recommended that Management, through the MPDO and the MBO, refrain from appropriating funds in lump sum and instead present itemized appropriations for specific development projects and activities, as embodied in the Municipality’s local development plan, or obtain approval from the Local Sanggunian before proceeding with the implementation of any project to be charged against the said appropriation pursuant to Article 454(d) of the IRR of the Local Government Code of 1991 (RA 7160) and Section 4(1) of P.D. No. 1445.

We further recommended that the Management send a formal communication to the Local Sanggunian detailing the utilization of the generic item in the appropriation ordinance for their information and/or ratification, to uphold the principles of transparency and integrity of government expenditure.

We recommended that Management, through the offices concerned, ensure strict compliance with the withholding and remittance of taxes, mandatory government deductions and contributions by:

promptly remitting intact all withheld funds and contributions during the month to the appropriate government agencies by the ensuing month, in faithful compliance with Section 69 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and other applicable laws, rules, and regulations; and
strengthening controls over the withholding and remittance process by conducting regular reconciliations, promptly recording necessary adjustments to reflect accurate balances, and addressing discrepancies as they arise.

We recommended the Management, through the BAC and other Municipal Offices, to ensure that all infrastructure projects undertaken by the Municipality are supported by duly approved CSHP in strict compliance with Section 5 of DOLE Department Order No. 13 series of 1998 Section 37.2.3 (f) of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 in order to protect the interest of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Management, through the BAC, ensure the posting of performance securities from winning bidders prior to the signing of contracts in strict compliance with Section 39.1 of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and to protect the interest of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management, through the concerned offices, ensure strict compliance with Section 4, Annex E, of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. 9184, and protect the interest of the Municipality by:

submitting to the Audit Team proof of payment of the advances made to the contractor which remained unrecouped;
strictly monitoring the advance payments made to contractors, and ensuring that all advances are systematically recouped through progress payments; and
taking prompt action to resolve the outstanding unrecouped advances by thoroughly reviewing contracts, verifying the securities submitted, confirming the actual worked accomplished, and assessing whether full recoupment is still feasible, without prejudice to the imposition of any other legal actions necessary for their settlement.

We commended Management for the substantial utilization of the LDRRMF in accordance with Republic Act No. 10121 leading to the attainment of the objectives of the fund.

Nevertheless, we recommended that Management, through the MDRRMO, optimize the utilization of the 5% LDRRMF by:

expediting the full implementation of the recently received funds amounting to ₱10,040,000.00, in accordance with the provisions of the Deed of Donation; and
ensuring that the PPAs funded under the 5% LDRRMF for each budget year are well planned and procurement- and implementation-ready, to mitigate, if not entirely prevent, delays in project execution.

We commended the Management and the concerned Departments for the efficient and effective planning for the timely implementation of PPAs resulting in the full implementation of 17 PPAs as of December 31, 2024, in accordance with DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020 and DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

Nevertheless, we recommended that Management, through the concerned offices, further enhance fund utilization by strictly monitoring the implementation of PPAs, especially those considered MOOE in nature, to ensure completion within the year and avoid budget lapses and implementation without valid appropriation.

We recommended the Management, through the Municipal Accounting Office, to exert earnest efforts in locating the documents and records of prior years and analyze the same to effect necessary adjustments in the books of accounts of the Municipality in compliance with Section 74 of PD No. 1445 and Sections 3.1, 3.2 and 3.3 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 and to present fairly the balance of the Cash in Bank - LCCA account in the Statement of Financial Position as of year-end.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
instruct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit an updated Certified List of Taxpayers, bearing the names, amount due and collectible during the year, and submit the same to the accounting unit as the basis in setting up of RPT/SEF tax receivable in the succeeding year; and
require the Municipal Accountant to use the Certified List of taxpayers prepared by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer as the basis in recording the RPT/SEF Tax Receivables at the beginning of the year, and to prioritize the preparation/maintenance of subsidiary ledgers for the respective accounts to ensure reliability of the RPT/SEF Tax Receivables account in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the General Services Office and Municipal Accounting Office and invite a representative of the DepEd Schools Division Office of Cagayan, to conduct a physical inventory of the aforementioned assets and prepare an Inventory Report containing all information about the existing and serviceable assets to be transferred with a net book value of ₱385,935.93.
Further, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive coordinate with the Head of the recipient Agency for proper turn-over of the assets. Thereafter, the Municipal Accounting Office shall draw the necessary Journal Entry Voucher to derecognize the PPE to be transferred with a net book value of P385,935.93, and furnish a copy of the JEV and supporting documents to the DepEd Schools Division Office of Cagayan as basis to record the same in their books of accounts.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee carefully perform detailed evaluation before post-qualification of eligible bids to ensure that computational errors and omissions are considered and to correctly identify the lowest calculated and responsive bid, in faithful adherence to Sections 32.2.1(b) and 32.2.4 of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184, and to avoid disallowance of transactions.

We recommended that Management ensure that warranty securities posted by contractors in procurement projects of the Municipality conform to the forms and amounts prescribed under Section 62.2.3.3 of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184 to ensure that it is protected against possible failures of contractors to comply with its responsibilities in the event of structural defects in infrastructure projects.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee ensure that Notices of Award are issued only after the completion of the post qualification procedures and performance securities are posted on or before the execution of the contract for the various procurement projects of the Municipality in strict compliance to Sections 37.1.1 and 37.2.1 of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184.

We recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office ensure that all payments are supported with complete documentary requirements prescribed under Item 9.1 and 9.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012 – 001 dated June 14, 2012.
Further, we recommended that the required lacking documents be submitted immediately to facilitate the review of the procurement and ensure the validity and propriety of the transactions as well as to avoid suspension in audit.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee ensure that duly recognized private groups and non-government organization be invited, in addition to the representative of the Commission on Audit, to sit as observers in the proceedings for the Municipality’s various procurement projects in faithful compliance to Section 13.1 of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184.

We recommended that Management fast track the implementation of the seven programs and projects funded under the 20% Development Fund, Trust Fund and loan proceeds for CY 2021 in the total amount of P21,728,713.04 to ensure that constituents are able to enjoy the benefits that they could derive from the programs and projects aimed at achieving socio-economic development and attaining desired Municipal objectives.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal DRRM Officer and other concerned implementing offices to fully utilize the available appropriations under the 70% Mitigation Fund and implement all budgeted projects, programs and activities within the planned period of completion to fully achieve its intended purposes.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the LDRRMO to prepare and submit a duly certified Report on Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund on or before the 15th day after end of each month through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council (LDC), to the COA Auditor of the LGU, copy furnished the OCD and the DILG-LGOO.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer be required to deposit all collections intact and promptly pursuant to Section 69 (1) of PD No. 1445 and applicable provisions of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the designated collecting officers prepare and submit the Monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms in compliance with applicable provisions of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for LGUs, Volume II.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Accountant ensure that disbursements for Representation and Transportation Allowance (RATA) are supported with the documentary requirements prescribed under Item 5.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Officials concerned to fast track the creation of a sex disaggregated database that will support the gender related issues identified and prioritized in the GAD Plan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the supporting schedules of the above-mentioned accounts as of December 31, 2020, otherwise appropriate sanctions will be imposed pursuant to existing rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accountant to: a) prioritize the establishment of subsidiary ledgers for the affected accounts; and b) reconcile and analyze the accounts to determine whether they warrant adjustment or write-off in accordance with existing COA rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Management require the Local School Board to submit the approved SEF Budget and Quarterly Reports of SEF utilization to the Office of the Auditor for review and audit, otherwise, the total disbursement of P795,868.57 for CY 2020 will be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly observe the provisions of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs in recording financial transactions of the Municipality and effect necessary adjusting entries to correct the misstatement in the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Management: (a) adopt an austere policy on the payment of representation and miscellaneous expenses, which is consistent with the provisions of law and COA rules and regulations; (b) ensure that payments for meals and snacks are actually incurred in the conduct of official business, reception, or entertainment; (c) require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all disbursements be supported with complete documentary evidence in compliance with Section 4(6) of P.D. No. 1445; and (d) refund the amount of P154,233.50 disallowed in audit since the same is considered as irregular disbursement of public funds.

We recommended that the Management stop the practice of payment thru reimbursement but to draw the check directly in the name of the suppliers or creditors in compliance with the provisions of Section 93 of PD No. 1445 and item 2.2 of COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly comply with Section 107 of PD 1445.  We likewise required the submission of the duly approved disbursement vouchers, together with the supporting documents. Otherwise, the said transactions shall be disallowed/suspended.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all fund transfers to NGAs be supported with complete documentary evidence in compliance with Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445 and Item 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001;
require the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking documents for verification and audit; and
instruct the Municipal Treasurer to require the schools to submit an official receipt issued by the DepEd DO-Cagayan for the release of financial assistance.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that disbursements for Representation and Transportation Allowance are supported with the documentary requirements prescribed in COA Circular 2012-001.  More importantly, Management should ensure that only Municipal officials and employees, who are not actually using government motor vehicle, be granted with the whole amount of RATA.

We recommended that the Management ensure that all payments are supported with complete documentary requirements prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012 – 001. Further, we required the immediate submission of the required lacking documents to facilitate the review of the procurement.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the Bids and Awards Committee to post on the GPPB Online Portal all negotiated procurements for Emergency Cases under RA 11469 in compliance with the pertinent provisions of GPPB Advisory No. 05-2020;
Require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all disbursements be supported with complete documentary evidence in compliance with Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445; and
Instruct the BAC and the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking documents for verification and audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and the BAC Chairman ensure that the required Project Procurement Management Plan and Annual Procurement Plan are prepared and submitted before any procurement of goods, services and civil works is undertaken to properly plan and monitor the procurement activities for the priority projects of the Municipality for a particular budget year.

We recommended that the Management submit to the Office of the Auditor the perfected contract agreements for the 4 infrastructure projects to avoid imposition of the penalty clause embodied in Item 4 of COA Circular 2009-001 and suspension in audit of payments made.

We recommended that the Management require all accountable officers to secure their Fidelity Bond in compliance with the provisions of PD 1445 and Treasury Circular 02 – 2009 in order that government may not be put on undue disadvantage when there are defalcations, shortages or unrelieved losses, as the case may be, in the accounts of the accountable public officers.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee include in the bidding documents for the procurement of infrastructure project, the Contractor’s All Risk Insurance as an additional requirement to be submitted by the winning bidder prior the execution of the Contract pursuant to the provisions of the General Conditions of the Contract of the Philippine Bidding Documents, 5th Edition and to ensure that the contractor’s risk is properly covered and readily enforceable.

We recommended that the Management require the contractor, Top Vision Design and Construction Services, to submit a Warranty Security after final acceptance of the completed project pursuant to the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184, to avoid audit suspension.

We recommended that the Management, through its Bids and Awards Committee, strictly observe the provisions of Republic Act No. 9184 in the determination of the method to be adopted by the Municipality in the procurement of goods and services.

We recommended that the Management, through its Bids and Awards Committee, strictly observe Section 54.1 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184 and ensure that splitting of requisitions in project implementation is avoided.
We further recommended that the Management submit an explanation why the subject procurements have been split, otherwise, the same will be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that the Procuring Entity and the BAC, through the BAC Secretariat, post the Notice of Award, Notice to Proceed and the approved contract within the prescribed period in the website of the LGU, if available, and in any conspicuous place in the premises of the LGU for transparency in the procurement activities.

We recommended that the BAC send invitation to observers in all aspects of the procurement process from the opening of bids conference down to the process of post-qualification. This is not only to comply with the requirement of the law but most of all to enhance transparency in the procurement activities of the LGU.
We recommended that the Management strictly observe the provisions of Republic Act No. 9184 on the recommended time frames of the procurement process.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer strictly monitor the accomplishment of work of the contractors to ensure the completion of the infrastructure projects of the LGU within the approved project duration.  Moreover, the Municipal Accountant should compute the correct amount of liquidated damages and collect it from the contractor.

We recommended that the Management, as well as the Bids and Awards Committee, strictly observe the provisions of Republic Act No. 9184 on the recommended time frames of the procurement process. We likewise recommended that the Management ensure that contracts entered into by the Agency with the contractors are duly notarized.

observations.managementAction:
Internal control being maintained by the Management in the Treasury Office is not sufficient to safeguard collections, accountable forms and records of the department. 

All accountable officers handling collections, accountable forms and payroll are properly bonded.

Although the drawers of the collecting officers are equipped with a lock, cubicles were not installed to provide privacy in their working area.

The Treasury Office maintains a permanent record book for all accountable forms.  However, no permanent Treasury personnel was appointed to update and maintain the said record.

The transactions are recorded by the Municipal Treasurer in the cashbook when the summary of all collections are prepared by a Treasury personnel, usually on a monthly basis.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the:
Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary journal entries to take up the depreciation expense for the current year, update the related Accumulated Depreciation accounts, as well as record adjustments in the government equity account for Prior Years’ depreciation expenses not previously recorded;
Municipal Accountant to review and reconcile the discrepancy noted on the balances of the PPE account as reported in the Statement of Financial Position as against the corresponding schedules, to fairly present the Financial Statements; and
Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer to:
Regularly conduct reconciliation of their reports so that any discrepancy noted could promptly adjusted and reconciled; and
Maintain the required PPE Ledger Cards, Real Property Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively.
observations.managementAction:
Some of the identified PPE accounts were depreciated.  However, PPE accounts, with a carrying amount of P33,337,481.39, were still not provided with allowance for depreciation.

Discrepancies were still noted on some balances of PPE accounts presented in the Statement of Financial Position versus the schedule of PPE that was submitted to the Audit Team.

PPELC, RPLC and property cards of the PPE of the Municipality were not maintained by the Accounting Office and the General Services Office.

The Municipal Treasurer provided the Accounting Office with the list of taxpayers, which was the basis of the Municipal Accountant in setting-up the RPT/SET Receivable accounts.

Items that were erroneously recorded in the Other Payables account were reclassified to the appropriate liability accounts.

From P1,980,326.82 in December 31, 2018, the dormant accounts have increased to P5,381,290.41 as of December 31, 2019.

Subsidiary ledgers and supporting schedules for Inventories, Other Payables, Receivables-Disallowances/Charges and Due to/from Other Funds accounts were prepared.

As for the Due to NGAs and Due to LGUs accounts, there are items presented in lump sum, where their breakdown are still not known.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the:
Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary journal entries to take up the depreciation expense for the current year, update the related Accumulated Depreciation accounts, as well as record adjustments in the government equity account for Prior Years’ depreciation expenses not previously recorded;
Municipal Accountant to review and reconcile the discrepancy noted on the balances of the PPE account as reported in the Statement of Financial Position as against the corresponding schedules, to fairly present the Financial Statements; and
Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer to:
regularly conduct reconciliation of their reports so that any discrepancy noted could promptly adjusted and reconciled; and
maintain the required Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC), Real Property Ledger Cards (RPLC) and Property Cards, respectively.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the accounts and make adjustments, if necessary, in order to reflect the correct balances of the Due to GSIS, Due to PHIC and Due to Pag-Ibig accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all fund transfers to NGAs and LGUs be supported with complete documentary evidence in compliance with Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445 and Item 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001. Further, we require that the lacking document be submitted for verification and audit.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the accounts and make adjustments, if necessary, in order to reflect the correct balance of the Due from LGUs account.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant properly classify the accounts in the financial statements so that it would be presented fairly in compliance with Section 113 of  PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant observe strict compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-02 dated September 11, 2012 on the Accounting and Reporting Guidelines for the LDRRMF of LGUs, specifically on the transfer of the unexpended/unobligated balance of the QRF and DRRMF-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund under the account “Trust Liability-DRRM” in the books.

We recommended that the Management:
set up receivable accounts for the tax deficiencies of contractors and use the Other Receivable account for this purpose; and
require the contractors to pay the tax deficiencies. If the contractor has an existing contract with the Municipal Government, deduct the tax deficiencies from their subsequent contract payments. Otherwise, the Municipal Government may institute legal actions to recover the amounts it had paid for tax deficiencies.

We recommended that the persons liable be required to settle their audit disallowances immediately, otherwise, on the basis of the COA Order of Execution or COE, their salaries and any money due them shall be withheld by the Municipal Treasurer for the settlement thereof.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive adopt strict measures to regulate the granting of cash advances in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular 97-002 dated February 10, 1997. Any unused or unspent cash advances should be returned or refunded.
We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly monitor the settlement of unliquidated cash advances and discontinue granting additional cash advances unless all outstanding cash advances have been liquidated. Failure to liquidate the cash advances within the prescribed period shall constitute a valid cause to withhold the salary of the grantee.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the concerned departments to strictly follow and implement the provisions contained in COA Circular No. 2015-008, to fairly present the book value of the Road Networks account in the Financial Statements of the Municipal Government.

We recommended that Management submit to the Office of the Auditor all perfected contract agreements/Purchase Orders within five (5) working days from the execution of the contract and to avoid imposition of the penalty clause embodied in Item 4 of COA Circular 2009-001.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the head GSO to mark the vehicles with “For Official Use Only” and to acquire government plates for all  motor vehicles of the LGU in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 77-61 to ensure the effective conservation of energy and proper utilization of government motor transportation.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare and submit the Monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms in compliance with the provisions of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for LGUs, Volume II.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deposit all collections intact and promptly pursuant to Section 69 (1) of PD No. 1445 and the provisions of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the Management ensure that all payments are supported with complete documentary requirements prescribed by COA Circular No. 2012 – 001. Further, we recommended that the lacking documents be submitted immediately to facilitate the review of the procurement.

We recommended that the Management strictly comply with the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 in the LGU’s procurement activities.  Moreover, the Bids and Awards Committee should exercise prudence in the examination of bidding documents submitted by prospective bidders to ensure that they are eligible to participate in the bidding and be awarded of the contract in faithful adherence to pertinent provisions of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) include in the bidding documents for the procurement of the LGU’s infrastructure project, the Contractor’s All Risk Insurance (CARI) as an additional requirement to be submitted by the winning bidder prior to the execution of the Contract pursuant to the provisions of the General Conditions of the Contract of the Philippine Bidding Documents, 5th Edition and to ensure that the contractor’s risk is properly covered and readily enforceable.

We recommended that Bids and Awards Committee ensure that the performance security is posted by the winning bidder within the prescribed period and in correct type, form, wording and validity period pursuant to Section 39.1 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184.

We recommended that the Management require TS Construction to submit the aforementioned documents to be part of its contract with the LGU in compliance with the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and to the stipulations in the Bid Data Sheet. We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee exercise prudence in the examination of bidding documents submitted by prospective bidders to ensure that they are eligible to participate in the bidding and be awarded of the contract in faithful adherence to pertinent provisions of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184.

We recommended that the Management strictly follow the provision of the above Circular to promote good governance, transparency and accountability and to accord the public of right and truthful information.

We recommended that the Management (a) harmonize the Municipality’s GAD’s plan with the Magna Carta of Women and the Philippine Development Plan in order to rationalize and widen the scope of programs/projects/activities in the ensuing years; and (b) convene the focal persons on GAD and require them to identify GAD Activities to address GAD issues and concerns and develop performance or success indicators for each GAD PPA.
